Name: Chocolatería San Ginés
Tips: Open 24 hours 365 days since 1894. Madrid's most iconic churros institution. Chocolate con porras + thick drinking chocolate for dipping. White-marble counter unchanged for decades. Empty at 08:00.

Name: Chocolatería de los Artesanos
Tips: Local rival to San Ginés since 1902. Same churros culture. Locals say the chocolate is thicker. Quieter mornings. Between Sol and Ópera.

Name: Café Comercial
Tips: Madrid's oldest café. Marble tables high ceilings mirrors. Where Pío Baroja and Antonio Machado argued politics. Reopened 2017 with period interior intact. Order café con leche + tostada con tomate or weekend brunch.

Name: HanSo Café
Tips: Pioneer of Madrid specialty-coffee wave. Best flat white in Madrid by common consensus. Exceptional croissants. Queue on weekend mornings — go before 09:30 on a weekday. Kouign-amann croissant.

Name: Toma Café
Address: C/ Olavide 3 (Toma 2) and C/ de la Palma 49 (Toma 1) 28004 Madrid (Malasaña)
Tips: The other half of Madrid specialty coffee since 2011. Expert baristas single-origin beans beautiful brew bar. Less crowded than HanSo. V60 pour-over espresso flight banana bread.

Name: Pum Pum Café
Tips: Lavapiés favourite specialty café. Bridgewater bean espresso sourdough breakfast plates leafy patio. Quieter than Malasaña spots. Avocado toast baked eggs flat white.

Name: La Mallorquina
Tips: Pastry institution since 1894 right on Puerta del Sol. Napolitanas de chocolate bambas de nata ensaimada mallorquina. Standing counter downstairs sit-down room upstairs with view over the plaza. Locals queue for take-away every morning.

Name: Pastelería Nunos
Tips: Repeatedly named Spain's best croissant by Madrid bakers guild (2017 2019 2024). Tiny neighbourhood shop in Chamberí. Go for morning croissant miss the HanSo queue. Butter croissant chocolate croissant.

Name: La Bicicleta Café
Tips: Malasaña hipster cliché done well. Bike-themed co-working by day brunch hub on weekends. Big tables eggs every way organic coffee. Good with kids — relaxed no rush. Huevos benedictinos pancakes flat white.

Name: El Riojano
Tips: Royal pastry shop since 1855 supplier to Spanish royal household. Beautiful preserved interior — marble counters dark wood vitrines painted ceiling. Pastas de té and pastelitos unchanged in 170 years.

Name: Mistura
Address: C/ del Barquillo 36 and 4 other locations 28004 Madrid
Tips: Madrid most-talked-about artisan gelato. Five locations citywide. Portuguese for mixture. You pick a flavour + add-ins they fold on cold marble in front of you. Fruit sorbets made from whole fresh fruit daily. Pistachio + sea salt is signature; basil sorbet is the surprise.

Name: Heladerías Palazzo
Address: C/ del Arenal 1 (Sol) C/ Goya 78 (Salamanca) C/ Princesa 26 + 6 others Madrid
Tips: Madrid institution since 1972 — Italian-style family-run multiple central locations. The classic gelato childhood memory for madrileños. Lemon and yogur natural are the locals' picks. The Arenal branch is 2 minutes from San Ginés.

Name: Giuseppe Ricci Gelato d'Italia
Tips: Genuine Italian gelato by Roman maestro Giuseppe Ricci. Often called the most authentic gelato in Madrid by Italian residents. Stracciatella and pistachio Bronte; chocolate sorbet is dairy-free and exceptional. Tiny — take it walking to Palacio Real.

Name: Rocambolesc
Address: Estación de Atocha + C/ Goya 35 + C/ Princesa 28 Madrid
Tips: Jordi Roca (El Celler de Can Roca 3-Michelin) ice-cream concept — soft-serve with theatrical topping bars. Photogenic fun for kids. The signature Wand (soft-serve dipped in chocolate caramelised hazelnuts).

Name: Helados Borneo
Tips: Hidden-gem Indonesian-Spanish husband-and-wife shop. Tropical fruit flavours (durian mangosteen jackfruit gula melaka soursop) you won't see elsewhere in Madrid. Walks straight to Reina Sofía.

Name: Los Alpes
Address: C/ de Arcipreste de Hita 6 28015 Madrid (Chamberí)
Tips: Chamberí classic since 1950. Old-school horchatería-heladería tiled walls same recipes for 75 years. The kind of place grandfathers bring grandchildren. Tarrina mixta is the format. Horchata fresca alongside in summer.

Name: Casa Dani
Address: Mercado de la Paz C/ de Ayala 28 28001 Madrid (Salamanca)
Tips: Legendary tortilla counter inside Mercado de la Paz. Queues form before opening (09:00). Tortilla española jugosa — creamy undercooked in centre — is one of the best in Spain. Cash only closed Sundays. WARNING often closed all of August — call to confirm reopening for Sep 1.

Name: Bodegas Rosell
Tips: Barrel-aged wine bar since 1920. Long communal marble counter old vermouth barrels blue-tile walls. Vermut de grifo (vermouth on tap) is one of the best in Madrid. Boquerones gildas anchovies. Authentic without trying. 10-min walk from the apt.

Name: Casa Toni
Tips: Cuchillería bar since 1989 — austere fluorescent-lit perfect. Famous for oreja a la plancha (grilled pig ear iconic Madrid offal tapa) and callos a la madrileña (tripe stew). For the adventurous.

Name: La Casa del Abuelo
Address: C/ de la Victoria 12 28012 Madrid (near Sol)
Tips: Famous for gambas al ajillo (sizzling prawns in garlic oil) since 1906. Original near Sol is best. Order with house sweet red wine (El Abuelo). Four locations.

Name: Casa Labra
Tips: Madrid tapas institution since 1860. The bar where the Spanish Socialist Party was founded in 1879 (plaque inside). Two windows: cash for tajadas de bacalao (battered cod) and croquetas; sit-down restaurant in back. THE Madrid tapas pilgrimage.

Name: Bar El Boquerón
Tips: Hidden Lavapiés cervecería specialising in fresh anchovies — boquerones en vinagre fried boquerones bocartes. Locals only. Cash.

Name: Cervecería Cervantes
Tips: Working-man's stand-up bar near Atocha — the gamba blanca de Huelva a la plancha is the order plus a caña plus a free tapa. Tiny packed at lunchtime in and out.

Name: Carambola
Tips: Family-friendly tapas restaurant on C/ Atocha — kids' menu patatas bravas pan con tomate pimientos de padrón. Relaxed well-suited to four with two children.

Name: El Brillante
Address: Plaza del Emperador Carlos V 8 28012 Madrid (Atocha)
Tips: The Atocha-station institution for bocadillo de calamares — Madrid's signature street food: crusty roll filled with fried squid rings lemon squeezed over. Cold Mahou alongside. €4 fed-and-watered.

Name: El Tigre
Tips: Chueca legend — order a caña (€2.50) and they bring a plate piled high with free tapas. Cheap loud crowded magic. Backpacker rite of passage; also where local students eat between exams.

Name: Sala de Despiece
Tips: High-concept butcher's-table on Calle Ponzano — staff in butcher's coats serve raw and just-cooked beef dishes. Standing counter queue theatrical. Carpaccio de gamba. Where Madrileños actually eat now.

Name: Tripea
Tips: Modern bites from a market stall counter; chef Roberto Martínez. Calle Ponzano cluster — where local crowds eat.

Name: Arallo Taberna
Tips: Galician seafood-tapas hybrid on Calle Ponzano. Pulpo a la plancha empanadas. Loud and packed at 21:00.

Name: Sobrino de Botín
Address: C/ de los Cuchilleros 17 28005 Madrid (La Latina)
Tips: In the Guinness Book as world's oldest restaurant (1725). 300-year wood-fired oven (never extinguished) roasts cochinillo asado (carved tableside) and cordero. 4-floor medieval cellar. Hemingway set the last scene of The Sun Also Rises here. Book 1–2 weeks ahead in summer.

Name: Taberna La Bola
Tips: Since 1870 — generations of same family still cooks cocido madrileño over wood fire in individual earthenware pots (pucheros). Bright red painted facade two blocks from Royal Palace. Three-stage cocido service. Cash only at lunch. Closed Sundays. Book +34 91 547 69 30.

Name: Casa Lucio
Tips: Beloved by Spanish royalty presidents Bill Clinton. The dish: huevos rotos con jamón — fried potatoes topped with runny fried eggs and Iberian ham smashed together at the table. Walls of celebrity photos. Reserve 1–2 weeks ahead.

Name: Casa Ciriaco
Tips: Since 1929 on C/ Mayor near the Royal Palace. Pepitoria de gallina (almond-saffron hen stew) is the signature — slow brown ancient. Quiet old-school dining room. Callos cocido (Wed only).

Name: Taberna El Sur
Address: C/ de la Torrecilla del Leal 12 28012 Madrid (Lavapiés)
Tips: Beloved Lavapiés neighbourhood restaurant — cocido madrileño served in stages alongside grilled meats and tortilla. Cheaper than the historic spots just as good. Reserve ahead for cocido.

Name: Casa González
Tips: Tiny ultramarinos (grocery + wine bar) near Reina Sofía. Floor-to-ceiling shelves of preserves cheeses wines; sit at a barrel and order a tasting board. Family-run since 1931. Quietly extraordinary.

Name: Posada de la Villa
Tips: Cavernous 17th-century inn on Cava Baja — wood-fired cordero asado (roast lamb) is the dish unchanged for 400 years. Slightly tourist-leaning but the lamb is the lamb.

Name: Corral de la Morería
Address: C/ de la Morería 17 28005 Madrid (La Latina)
Tips: Most celebrated tablao in the world. Where every flamenco great — Antonio Gades Camarón Lola Flores — has performed. Tiny stage 60 seats audience 3m from dancers. Two rooms: Tablao (show + drinks) and Michelin-starred Gastronómico (tasting menu + show).

Name: Cardamomo
Tips: Critics' pick for authentic flamenco (working artists not tourist troupe). Three shows nightly (18:00 20:00 22:30). 20:00 sitting works for kids. Smaller more intimate than the grand tablaos.

Name: Tablao Flamenco 1911
Tips: Formerly Villa Rosa — oldest active tablao in the world (since 1911). Closed and rebranded 2020. Beautiful tiled interior on Plaza Santa Ana. Less star-power than Corral but building and lineage are extraordinary.

Name: Las Tablas
Tips: Less famous less expensive very good. Two daily shows decent dinner. The budget right answer for families.

Name: Mercado de San Miguel
Address: Plaza de San Miguel 1 28005 Madrid (La Latina)
Tips: Stunning 1916 iron-and-glass food hall next to Plaza Mayor. ~20 stalls: oysters jamón ibérico croquetas vermouth on tap sushi patisserie. Best before noon or after 22:00.

Name: Mercado de San Antón
Tips: Chueca 3-floor food market — ground produce 1st-floor tapas counters rooftop bar with city views. Far fewer tourists than San Miguel. Mistura gelato on ground floor.

Name: Mercado de la Paz
Tips: Salamanca working neighbourhood market — old-school stalls of butchers cheese fishmongers. Home to Casa Dani tortilla counter. Less polished than San Miguel; more real.

Name: Mercado de Antón Martín
Address: C/ de Santa Isabel 5 28012 Madrid (Las Letras)
Tips: Las Letras working market — produce butchers plus cluster of small restaurants on upper floor (Japanese Venezuelan Peruvian Italian). Working-people's lunch spot. Cheap fast varied.

Name: Mercado de Vallehermoso
Tips: Chamberí working market — local lively hardly any tourists. Tapas stalls inside. Best on a Saturday morning. Bocadillo de tortilla; oysters at Maricastaña.

Name: Platea Madrid
Tips: 1940s cinema in Salamanca converted into vast 3-floor gastronomic complex — celebrity-chef stalls cocktail bars a stage. Stunning architecture decent food more spectacle than substance but the building is worth seeing.

Name: Antigua Pastelería del Pozo
Tips: Madrid's oldest bakery — since 1830. Hand-painted ceiling mahogany counters tiled floor original wood-fired oven. Empanadas de bonito roscones bartolillos. Untouched in 195 years. Closed weekends.

Name: La Violeta
Tips: Since 1915. The shop that invented violet candies — sugared crystallised violet flowers in tiny tin boxes. The favourite sweet of King Alfonso XIII. Buy a tin as a present.

Name: Casa Mira
Tips: Since 1855 on Carrera de San Jerónimo. The Madrid temple of turrón (Christmas almond nougat) — they sell it year-round. Beautiful preserved shopfront.

Name: Convento del Corpus Christi (Las Carboneras)
Address: Plaza del Conde de Miranda 3 28005 Madrid
Tips: Cloistered nuns sell home-made pastas and cookies through a turnstile in the wall — you can't see them they can't see you. Buzz the bell on Plaza Conde de Miranda. Surreal hidden intensely Madrid. Surprisingly good pastas de almendra + mantecadas.

Name: Helados Acueducto
Tips: Genuine Italian gelato 2-min walk from the Segovia Aqueduct — perfect post-cochinillo cool-down. Stracciatella limón pistachio.
